claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots create file digest maps before/after agent execution to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation detects project file changes.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot taken before agents, diff after execution, violations generate blockers that prevent PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of boundary audit blocks mutations.

evidence.summary: Execution policy explicitly declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots as artifact directory only.
evidence.why: Policy documents the boundary contract enforced by the audit.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules forbid modifying target project files and require evidence scope declarations.
evidence.why: Evidence agents are instructed not to mutate projects.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, ensuring project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Classification enforces evidence requirements for existing project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators.
evidence.why: Enforcement prevents assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates blockers when evidence records omit required scope field.
requiredEvidenceRoles returns explorer, cross-checker, verifier for cross_check policy to force independent verification.
evidence.why: Cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on high-risk claims.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement works.

evidence.summary: Tests verify external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and scope-less evidence is blocked.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves evidence scope enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Protocol contract requires evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with configurable tool profiles for evidence agent execution.
evidence.why: Provider abstraction enables full-tool or boundary-restricted agent spawning.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes providers with timeout, captures stdout/stderr, and detects budget exceeded failures.
evidence.why: Provider runtime handles evidence agent execution and failure classification.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos, agents could corrupt projects without blockers.
If claude provider ignores tool profile restrictions, full-tool agents might mutate projects despite boundary policy.
If evidence scope classification misclassifies external URLs as project, unverified claims could become facts.

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary audit exists, is tested, and blocks PRD readiness on mutations or missing scope. Cross-check protocol forces independent verification for high-risk claims.
